sources.notes: Construct a permutation of length n with a required inversion-related value k, or report impossible.
Official reference code runs a partition DP over block sizes and reconstructs consecutive increasing blocks in descending block order.

annotation.summary: Construct a permutation whose inversion value equals k, or output 0 if no such permutation exists.
annotation.constraints: n is small enough for DP over all possible inversion values up to n(n-1)/2.
annotation.core_idea: View the permutation as consecutive increasing blocks, arranged so every element of an earlier block is larger than every element of a later block. A block of size y contributes C(y,2) to the complement of the target cross-block inversion value, so the problem becomes partitioning n into block sizes whose C(y,2) sum is n(n-1)/2-k. A knapsack-style DP records whether a prefix length and complement value are reachable, then the block sizes reconstruct the permutation.
annotation.complexity: O(n^2 * n(n-1)/2) in the direct DP used by the official solution.

solution_variants.name: block-partition-dp
solution_variants.summary: DP over total length and complement value to choose block sizes, then output increasing consecutive blocks in descending value ranges.

tags.evidence: The solution chooses block sizes whose triangular-number contributions sum to the required complement.
After the DP proves reachability, the answer is built explicitly from the recovered block sizes.
The key observation is a structured permutation made of increasing blocks ordered by decreasing value ranges.
